name: brief-latency-spike-recovered
description: 15-minute latency spike (p95 18s vs normal 4s), 8 traces. Correlates with long conversations. Back to normal since.

item.title: Latency spike on customer-support-bot (recovered)
item.description: Automated alert: p95 latency exceeded 15s threshold on customer-support-bot between 09:15-09:30 UTC.
item.note:
Investigated the latency spike. Findings:

- Spike window: 09:15-09:30 UTC (15 minutes)
- 8 traces in the window, latency breakdown:
  - brk-1101: 18.2s, brk-1102: 16.4s, brk-1103: 14.8s, brk-1104: 17.1s
  - brk-1105: 15.9s, brk-1106: 12.3s, brk-1107: 19.1s, brk-1108: 16.8s
- p95 during spike: 18.7s (vs normal 4.0s baseline)
- p50 during spike: 16.6s
- All 8 traces completed successfully (no errors)

Correlation analysis:
- All 8 traces had conversation_length > 12 turns (metadata field)
- Average input tokens: 48,200 (vs normal ~12,000)
- The LLM call spans took 12-15s each (vs normal 1.5-2.5s)
- Retrieval spans were normal (~200ms)
- The long conversations appear to come from a single user session (session-id: sess-8842) doing iterative troubleshooting

Recovery:
- 09:30 UTC onwards: p95 dropped to 4.2s immediately
- 09:30-10:30 UTC: 22 traces, all normal latency, p50 2.7s, p95 3.9s
- No further spikes as of 12:00 UTC check

The spike was caused by unusually long conversations hitting the LLM with large context windows. It's resolved, but worth monitoring whether this pattern (long sessions causing latency spikes) recurs.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
